title: allium phanerantherum subsp. phanerantherum
intro: Allium phanerantherum subsp. phanerantherum is a subspecies of wild garlic, scientifically known as a type of Allium, commonly referred to as Phan's wild garlic or Phan's wild onion. It is a type of perennial plant, native to the mountains of China and parts of Asia, that has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties and health benefits. The plant is valued for its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat various health issues, including colds, fever, and skin conditions. Traditionally, the plant is prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and salves, which are used to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
